title: Elections for the government's chief executive
subtitle: Data by the Lexical Index of Electoral Democracy.
caption:
In 2025, the global map of elections for the government's chief executive divides countries into those holding such elections and those that do not, based on the Lexical Index of Electoral Democracy. The visualization uses a binary color scale where blue indicates the presence of an election for the chief executive and orange indicates its absence, with grey hatching marking regions where data is unavailable.

The blue category covers the majority of the world's landmass and population. This includes the entirety of the Americas, from Canada and the United States down through Brazil and Argentina. In Europe, the blue band encompasses the United Kingdom, France, Germany, Spain, and the Nordic countries, extending east to include Ukraine and Russia. In Asia, India, Japan, South Korea, and Indonesia fall into this group, as does Australia in Oceania. Most of sub-Saharan Africa is also colored blue, including Nigeria, the Democratic Republic of the Congo, and South Africa.

The orange category, representing countries without an election for the chief executive in 2025, forms distinct regional clusters. In the Middle East and North Africa, this includes Morocco, Algeria, Libya, Egypt, Saudi Arabia, and Iran. In Asia, China and Myanmar are shown in orange. In Africa, Sudan and Madagascar also appear in this band. Greenland is the only large landmass marked with the grey "No data" pattern.

The distribution shows a clear geographic pattern where the absence of chief executive elections is concentrated in North Africa, the Middle East, and parts of East and Southeast Asia, while the practice is widespread across the Americas, Europe, and most of the rest of the world.
